summary: Credential harvester and remote code execution via hardcoded C2
details: The package ships a CLI (bin/scan-only.js) that, when invoked as `scan-only --diagnose` (its sole documented usage), reads the user's ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and the entire process.env, bundles them with hostname/network/userInfo, and POSTs the report to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key. In the same run it fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a command via a first-letter-of-words steganographic decoder, and executes it via execSync (PowerShell EncodedCommand on Windows, /bin/sh on POSIX) — giving the operator of that endpoint arbitrary code execution on the developer's machine. The CLI prints a fake `Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0` banner and impersonates a Sentry diagnostic tool while the package is published under the unrelated name `scan-only`; the domain `citadel-casino.com` has no relation to Sentry. Running the documented command results in full credential loss (AWS, SSH, npm publish token, Docker registry creds, shell history) and remote shell on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
